senses.gloss: to be hindmost, to come behind, to follow
to have galls in the back, to have sores in the back
to arrange, to dispose, to plan, to prepare, to organize, to hatch, to contrive, to devise, to forecast

examples.text: يُدَبِّرُ الْأَمْرَ مِنَ السَّمَاءِ إِلَى الْأَرْضِ ثُمَّ يَعْرُجُ إِلَيْهِ فِي يَوْمٍ كَانَ مِقْدَارُهُ أَلْفَ سَنَةٍ مِّمَّا تَعُدُّونَ
examples.english: He arranges [each] matter from the heaven to the earth; then it will ascend to Him in a Day, the extent of which is a thousand years of those which you count.

senses.gloss: to direct, to manage, to regulate

etymology.text: From Proto-Afroasiatic *dâb-. Compare دُبْر (dubr, “back”), perhaps cognate with Hebrew דִּבֵּר (“to speak”).
